title: Layer thickness dependent band gap of MBE grown single- to few-layer MoS$_{2}$
authors: Maciej Bazarnik, Thorsten Deilmann, Marta Przychodnia, Anika Schlenhoff
abstract: In light of the rise of transition metal dichalcogenides as 2D semiconductors for device applications, band engineering becomes very important from an application point of view. In many of these materials, such as the canonical example of MoS$_{2}$, the semiconductor band gap depends on the layer number. It changes from indirect to direct as it evolves from a bulk semiconductor to a monolayer. Interestingly, it was predicted and experimentally confirmed that, by thinning the material from bulk to a bilayer, the indirect transition shows a strong blue-shift. Here, we present the results of scanning tunnelling spectroscopy measurements on MoS$_{2}$ that has been grown \textit{in situ} via molecular beam epitaxy on graphene on Ir(111) at thicknesses ranging from 1 to 5 layers. We find a drastic decrease of the band gap with increasing layer number, to values even below the band gap in bulk. We also observe that the pinning of the conduction band vanishes above 4 layers. Comparing our experimental data with density functional theory and \textit{GW} calculations indicates that an additional screening is introduced by the sample growth conditions.
summary: The study tracks how the electronic band gap of MoS2 changes as it grows from a single layer to five layers. It reveals that the band gap drops below the known bulk value due to extra electronic screening caused by defects like grain boundaries and stacking faults inherent in the growth process.
main_problem: Investigating the layer-thickness dependence of the electronic band gap in MoS2 from monolayer to 5 layers and determining why the 5-layer band gap is lower than the bulk value.
main_result: A drastic decrease in the band gap is observed as layer number increases, reaching values below the bulk limit, which is attributed to additional screening from growth-induced defects.
method: Scanning Tunneling Spectroscopy (STS) was used for experimental measurement, while Density Functional Theory (DFT) and GW calculations were used for theoretical comparison.
model_or_system: MoS2 layers (1 to 5 atomic layers) grown via Molecular Beam Epitally on a graphene/Ir(111) substrate using a Stranski-Krastanov growth mode.
key_observables: Band gap (Eg), conduction band and valence band edge positions relative to the Fermi level, and dI/dU differential conductance.
important_parameters: Layer thickness (1-5L), temperature (5.5 K), and screening/doping weights.
main_assumptions: The theoretical model assumes a defect-free experimental structure and uses empirically estimated doping weights to match experimental data.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows STM topographies of different coverage regimes (1-2L, 2-4L, and 3-5L); Figure 2 shows normalized dI/dU curves and extracted band gap data.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the importance of band engineering in TMDs, describes the MBE growth and STM/STS experimental setup, presents the thickness-dependent band gap measurements, compares results with DFT/GW calculations, and concludes with a discussion on the role of growth-induced screening.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
